Empires & Puzzles Costumed Rudolph Review: The Armored Reindeer Strikes Back

Santa's favorite steed has traded his jingle bells for plate mail. With an attack stat that rivals 4-stars and a nasty mana cut, is Costumed Rudolph the new king of 3-star tournaments? Or is he just shiny fodder?
Costumed Rudolph: From Nose So Bright to Armor So Tight
Rudolph has returned, and this time he's not just guiding the sleigh—he's armored up to defend it. The

costume transforms the fragile classic hero into a modern, hard-hitting menace. Released as part of the 2025 Winter Event update, this costume breathes new life into one of the oldest event heroes in the game.

Gone is the cute, vulnerable fawn. Costumed Rudolph is decked out in sleek, silver plate armor with red gem accents and festive barding. He looks determined, battle-ready, and slightly ridiculous in the best way possible. It’s a great visual upgrade that tells you: "I'm done playing games."
The costume brings a massive overhaul to Rudolph's stats:
This is huge. For context, modern 3-star hitters like
sit around 559 Attack. Rudolph's attack is elite for a 3-star, allowing him to punch well above his weight class.
Still his Achilles' heel. While improved over the base version, 477 is considered low in the current meta (where 500+ is standard).
A decent improvement, but he remains a "glass cannon"—high damage, low durability.

Combined with his 648 Attack stat, this hits
. He is a legitimate sniper at the 3-star level.
An upgrade from the base 20%. It cuts nearly a quarter of the enemy's mana bar, which is often enough to delay a slow hero by several turns or completely reset a fast one.
This attempts to fix his low defense stat. While 24% isn't game-breaking, it might help him survive a slash attack that would otherwise kill him.
Still the weak 10% minion, but now serves as a delivery system for his passive frost damage.
295% of 648 Attack = ~1,911 raw damage value. This is a ~24% increase in raw damage output compared to his base form.
244 Frost damage over 4 turns = 61 damage per turn. If the minion survives to hit even once, it adds a nice DOT layer.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Winter Family bonus is standard (minion stats), but the real star here is the Costume Bonus stats and the new passive.
This is excellent for long fights. Even though the minion is weak, if it lands a hit, the enemy takes guaranteed Frost damage. It adds pressure and makes ignoring him risky.

Unlike the base version's "Attack Up," the costume gets a defensive boost. This is actually smart design; he already has massive attack, so shoring up his weak defense helps him live long enough to use it.
Base Rudolph faded into obscurity years ago. This costume is a deliberate attempt to bring him back to the table.
With 648 Attack, he is statistically relevant again. He hits harder than many Season 5 and 6 three-stars. His mana cut remains a premium skill that hasn't been devalued by time.
He is a strong contender for red stacks in 3-star tournaments, particularly in
where his mana control and direct damage shine, and healing isn't an option.
The Costume is superior in every way except potentially the class (Barbarian vs Fighter).
While Revive is great for squishy heroes, the sheer stat difference (nearly +100 Attack) makes the Costume the clear winner. You want him to kill the enemy, not revive to die again.
The massive stat boost from the costume bonus is what makes this hero playable.
Leans into his strength. With high attack tiles and a high % special, you want to maximize damage.

This costume turns a "meh" hero into a "must-level" for your 3-star roster. Prioritize him if you lack fast snipers. He will carry you through early map stages and help you place higher in tournaments.

Don't sleep on him just because he's old. That 648 attack stat is legitimate. He is a cheap, effective option for depth in 3-star events and a great tile-booster for Mythic Titans if you are restricted to 3-stars.
"I’d rather have Ginger’s costume than Noel... But wait, Robo Rudolph looks cool."
- A content creator weighing the aesthetics.
"The new one... it's an extra 10% damage and a extra 3% on the mana reduction... very unimaginative."
- Some players felt the upgrade was "safe" rather than exciting.
"I honestly go in and look if I have some slow speed heroes with costumes... Rudolph is just a single target sniper."
- The debate continues on whether single-target snipers are enough in the modern AOE meta.

Costumed Rudolph is the definition of a "glow-up." He hasn't reinvented the wheel—he's just made it spin faster and hit harder. While he lacks the flashiness of new mechanics, reliable fast mana control paired with elite tile damage makes him a solid workhorse. He isn't broken, but he's definitely fixed.
